computer_science_courses.title: Computer Systems Architecture
computer_science_courses.description: Introduction to computer organization and architecture including processor design, memory hierarchy, and input/output systems. Students learn how hardware and software interact while understanding performance optimization and system-level programming concepts.
learning_objectives: Understand computer organization and architectural components
Analyze processor design and instruction execution
Evaluate memory hierarchy and storage systems
Implement assembly language programming
Optimize code for hardware performance characteristics

computer_science_courses.title: Database Management Systems
computer_science_courses.description: Comprehensive study of database design, implementation, and management. Students learn relational database concepts, SQL programming, and database administration while understanding data modeling and database optimization techniques.
learning_objectives: Design efficient relational database schemas
Write complex SQL queries for data manipulation
Implement database normalization and optimization
Understand transaction management and concurrency control
Apply database security and backup strategies

computer_science_courses.title: Operating Systems
computer_science_courses.description: Study of operating system principles including process management, memory management, file systems, and system security. Students learn how operating systems manage computer resources and provide services to applications.
learning_objectives: Understand operating system architecture and components
Analyze process scheduling and synchronization
Evaluate memory management strategies
Implement file system operations and security
Configure and optimize operating system performance

computer_science_courses.title: Web Development
computer_science_courses.description: Comprehensive introduction to web development technologies and frameworks. Students learn to create dynamic, responsive web applications using modern front-end and back-end technologies while following web development best practices.
learning_objectives: Develop responsive web applications using HTML, CSS, and JavaScript
Implement client-server architecture and APIs
Use modern web development frameworks and libraries
Apply web security best practices
Deploy and maintain web applications

computer_science_courses.title: Computer Networks
computer_science_courses.description: Study of computer networking principles, protocols, and architectures. Students learn about network design, data communication, and network security while understanding how distributed systems communicate over various network infrastructures.
learning_objectives: Understand network protocols and communication models
Analyze network performance and reliability
Design and configure network infrastructures
Implement network security measures
Troubleshoot network connectivity issues
covered_materials: Quality of Service (QoS) and traffic management

computer_science_courses.title: Machine Learning Applications
computer_science_courses.description: Practical application of machine learning algorithms and techniques to real-world problems. Students learn to implement, train, and evaluate machine learning models while understanding data preprocessing and model selection strategies.
learning_objectives: Implement supervised and unsupervised learning algorithms
Preprocess and prepare data for machine learning
Evaluate and compare machine learning models
Apply machine learning to domain-specific problems
Use machine learning libraries and frameworks

computer_science_courses.title: Cybersecurity Principles
computer_science_courses.description: Introduction to cybersecurity concepts and practices for software developers. Students learn secure coding practices, vulnerability assessment, and security testing while understanding how to build secure software systems.
learning_objectives: Apply secure coding practices in software development
Identify and mitigate common security vulnerabilities
Implement authentication and authorization mechanisms
Conduct security testing and code reviews
Understand regulatory compliance requirements

computer_science_courses.title: Mobile Application Development
computer_science_courses.description: Development of mobile applications for iOS and Android platforms. Students learn mobile development frameworks, user interface design for mobile devices, and mobile-specific programming concepts including platform guidelines and app store deployment.
learning_objectives: Develop native and cross-platform mobile applications
Design mobile user interfaces following platform guidelines
Implement mobile-specific features and APIs
Optimize applications for mobile performance
Deploy applications to app stores

computer_science_courses.title: Cloud Computing
computer_science_courses.description: Introduction to cloud computing concepts, services, and deployment models. Students learn to design, implement, and manage cloud-based solutions while understanding scalability, reliability, and cost optimization in cloud environments.
learning_objectives: Understand cloud computing models and services
Design scalable cloud architectures
Implement cloud-based applications and services
Manage cloud security and compliance
Optimize cloud costs and performance

computer_science_courses.title: Data Science and Analytics
computer_science_courses.description: Application of statistical methods and computational techniques to extract insights from large datasets. Students learn data analysis, visualization, and interpretation while using programming tools and statistical software for data science projects.
learning_objectives: Apply statistical methods to analyze complex datasets
Create effective data visualizations and reports
Use programming tools for data manipulation and analysis
Implement data mining and pattern recognition techniques
Communicate data-driven insights to stakeholders

computer_science_courses.title: Human-Computer Interaction
computer_science_courses.description: Study of interaction between humans and computer systems focusing on usability, user experience, and interface design. Students learn to evaluate and design user interfaces while understanding cognitive and social factors in human-computer interaction.
learning_objectives: Understand principles of human-computer interaction
Design user-centered interfaces and experiences
Conduct usability testing and evaluation
Apply accessibility principles in interface design
Evaluate emerging interaction technologies

computer_science_courses.title: Advanced Programming Concepts
computer_science_courses.description: Advanced programming techniques and paradigms including functional programming, concurrent programming, and software design patterns. Students explore multiple programming languages and develop expertise in advanced software development practices.
learning_objectives: Master advanced programming paradigms and techniques
Implement concurrent and parallel programming solutions
Apply software design patterns effectively
Compare and contrast different programming languages
Develop high-performance and maintainable code

computer_science_courses.title: Software Testing and Quality Assurance
computer_science_courses.description: Comprehensive study of software testing methodologies, quality assurance practices, and software reliability. Students learn to design test plans, implement automated testing, and ensure software quality throughout the development lifecycle.
learning_objectives: Design comprehensive software test plans and strategies
Implement automated testing frameworks
Apply various testing methodologies and techniques
Evaluate software quality metrics and standards
Manage defect tracking and resolution processes

mathematics_courses.title: Discrete Mathematics
mathematics_courses.description: Mathematical structures and concepts fundamental to computer science including logic, set theory, combinatorics, and graph theory. Students develop mathematical reasoning skills essential for algorithm analysis and computer science applications.
learning_objectives: Apply formal logic and proof techniques
Solve combinatorial and counting problems
Analyze graph theory problems and algorithms
Understand mathematical foundations of computer science
Develop mathematical reasoning and proof skills

mathematics_courses.title: Calculus I
mathematics_courses.description: Introduction to differential and integral calculus with applications. Students learn limits, derivatives, and integrals while developing problem-solving skills essential for advanced mathematics and scientific applications.
learning_objectives: Evaluate limits and understand continuity
Calculate derivatives using various techniques
Apply derivatives to optimization and related rate problems
Evaluate definite and indefinite integrals
Solve real-world problems using calculus concepts

mathematics_courses.title: Linear Algebra
mathematics_courses.description: Study of vector spaces, linear transformations, and matrix operations with applications to computer science and engineering. Students learn concepts essential for computer graphics, machine learning, and data analysis.
learning_objectives: Perform matrix operations and solve linear systems
Understand vector spaces and linear transformations
Calculate eigenvalues and eigenvectors
Apply linear algebra to computer science problems
Use linear algebra in data analysis and machine learning

additional_business_courses.title: Financial Accounting
additional_business_courses.description: Fundamental principles of financial accounting including the accounting cycle, financial statement preparation, and basic accounting concepts. Students learn to record, classify, and summarize business transactions while understanding financial reporting requirements.
learning_objectives: Understand fundamental accounting principles and concepts
Record business transactions using double-entry bookkeeping
Prepare basic financial statements
Analyze financial information for decision-making
Apply accounting standards and ethical principles

additional_business_courses.title: Managerial Accounting
additional_business_courses.description: Application of accounting information for internal management decision-making including cost analysis, budgeting, and performance evaluation. Students learn to use accounting data for planning, controlling, and improving organizational performance.
learning_objectives: Apply cost accounting concepts and methods
Prepare budgets and performance reports
Analyze variance and cost behavior
Use accounting information for decision-making
Evaluate performance measurement systems
